exemplar.title: To what extent should ARK introduce an ETF that includes Harry Markowitzâs Portfolio Selection Theory as a growth strategy?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent should ARK introduce an ETF that includes Harry Markowitzâs Portfolio Selection Theory as a growth strategy?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: The topic is communicated accurately and effectively with comprehensive explanation throughout the essay
The research question is clearly stated, well-focused and consistently linked to every analytical section
An appropriate and well-justified range of secondary sources and methods are selected with clear evidence of informed planning
weaknesses: The methodology lacks some practical execution details (no specification of computational tools or software)
Justification for certain methodological choices (e.g. three-year risk horizon) is underdeveloped
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of Focus and Method perform at the top of the 5â6 band: the introduction and conclusion maintain a clear, comprehensive thread; the sharply focused research question is consistently connected; and the selection of sources and business tools shows strong, informed planning. Although minor methodological details are missing, these do not undermine the overall coherence and precision.

strengths: Consistent and accurate use of advanced financial terminology and concepts (e.g. âmeanâvariance optimisation,â âsystematic riskâ)
Clear and coherent subject knowledge of Modern Portfolio Theory, the Ansoff matrix, and ETF mechanics
Generally relevant and appropriate application of source material, with concrete examples (e.g. 60/40 asset allocation)
weaknesses: Application of source material is uneven: some explanations rely on generic definitions rather than rigorous data
Lacks numerical back-testing or deeper empirical illustration of the proposed portfolio to reach the highest band
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Subject knowledge is clear and coherent, and terminology is used accurately throughout, meriting the upper band. Application of sources is mostly relevant, with strong citations, but uneven depth and absence of back-testing prevent attainment of full marks.

strengths: Research quality is good, with a broad set of quantitative and qualitative secondary sources directly relevant to the research question
Analysis moves beyond description, applying position maps and the Ansoff matrix to generate supported conclusions
Discussion acknowledges alternative viewpoints and critically evaluates key assumptions of Modern Portfolio Theory
weaknesses: Depth of analysis is limited by the absence of executed optimisation calculations or sensitivity testing
Evaluation is only partially critical: it recognises MPTâs limitations but does not propose concrete next steps or future-research suggestions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality clearly exceeds the mid-band and analysis is well supported, justifying a solid mark in the 7â9 range; however, the limited practical execution of quantitative methods and the largely qualitative evaluation constrain it from a higher adjustment, placing the overall score at the upper end of the mid band.

strengths: All required structural elements (title page, table of contents, sections, references) are present
Logical progression of headings mirrors the analytical argument, aiding navigation
weaknesses: Multiple formatting inconsistencies: duplicate table-of-contents entries, irregular list numbering, abrupt sentence truncations
Mixed citation styles, inconsistent mathematical formatting (plain-text vs. LaTeX), and occasional first-person language undermine polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the reportâs overall structure meets IB requirements, numerous layout and formatting errors significantly detract from readability and professional presentation, placing this criterion in the lower band.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is stated verbatim on the title page and restated in the introduction.  Its wording ("To what extent should ARK introduce an ETF that includes Harry Markowitz's Portfolio Selection Theory as a growth strategy?") is sharply focused, contains a clear command term, and remains a tight thread that links every analytical section (position map, MPT application, Ansoff matrix) to the final judgement.  No noticeable drift or broadening occurs, so the focus is maintained effectively across 3 700 words.

Arguments are logically structured and alternative viewpoints (e.g., ARKKâs under-performance might be temporary) are acknowledged.  The evaluation addresses limitations of MPT and risks within the Ansoff quadrants, demonstrating partial critical engagement.  However, evaluative comments tend to be qualitative and would benefit from firmer quantitative backing, keeping the work within the mid-high band rather than the top.

The essay displays clear and coherent knowledge of Modern Portfolio Theory, the Ansoff growth matrix, and ETF mechanics.  The student accurately explains variance, covariance and efficient frontier concepts, integrates them into the ARK context, and recognises real-world limitations of MPT.  The treatment is robust, though still theoretical in parts, fitting solidly within the upper band.

Financial and business-management terminology (e.g., "meanâvariance optimisation", "systematic risk", "market penetration") is used accurately and consistently.  Formula notation is largely correct and symbols are defined immediately beneath equations.  Minor typographical slips remain, but these do not impede understanding, so the work meets the descriptor for accurate and consistent use of concepts.

Analysis moves beyond description by juxtaposing ARKKâs position map results with competitors, applying MPT formulas conceptually, and employing the Ansoff matrix to weigh strategic avenues.  Conclusions are generally justified, yet the lack of executed optimisation calculations and sensitivity testing limits depth.  Thus, analysis is clear and largely supported but not fully sophisticated.

Citations from Morningstar, Yahoo Finance and academic journals are woven directly into the analytical narrative (e.g., risk scores and YTD returns in the position map).  Nevertheless, some Investopedia-style definitions are used where more rigorous financial data could strengthen the argument, and numerical back-testing of the proposed ETF is absent.  Overall application is mostly relevant and appropriate, but uneven depth prevents it from reaching the highest band.

An extensive range of current, reputable secondary sources (Morningstar data sets, academic articles, Financial Times, CFA resources) is deployed and, in most cases, chosen with a clear purpose.  The methods (position map, meanâvariance calculations, Ansoff matrix) are appropriate for the RQ.  While the study still relies solely on secondary data and the quantitative model is not fully executed with numerical outputs, the selection shows planning and informed choice, justifying a strongâthough not flawlessâperformance in this strand.

The student assembles a broad set of up-to-date quantitative and qualitative sources and keeps them firmly connected to the research question.  Although primary research is absent, each data set (risk scores, return figures, capital-outflow figures) is germane to assessing ARKâs strategic options, indicating good overall research quality.

Basic layout conventionsâmargins, line spacing, and use of headingsâare met, and visual aids are embedded with captions.  Nonetheless, inconsistencies persist: duplicated table-of-contents entries, irregular indentation, and mixed citation styles.  These issues compromise polish, situating the work in the middle band for formatting accuracy.

The student opens with a concise, well-structured introduction that clearly situates the business context (ARKKâs recent under-performance) and the academic theory (Markowitzâs MPT).  Key terms such as âriskâreturn profileâ and âefficient frontierâ are defined in-text, and the rationale for investigating a new ETF is explained logically and without ambiguity.  Throughout the essay the topic remains front-and-centre and is revisited explicitly in the conclusion, demonstrating comprehensive and accurate communication.

All compulsory EE structural elements are present (title page, abstract/intro, table of contents, main body, conclusion, references, appendices).  Headings follow a logical progression that mirrors the analytical path, aiding navigation.  Despite minor duplication in the contents table, the overall structure is appropriate and fully meets IB requirements.

annotations.comment: The methodology does not specify which computational tools or software will be used, leaving practical execution details undefined.
The covariance formula is presented in plain text rather than LaTeX, creating inconsistency in mathematical formatting.
The 60%/40% asset allocation example concretely demonstrates how the theory informs the design of ARKConservative.
The student provides a clear word count at the start of the document, meeting the IB formatting requirement for extended essays.
The title of the investigation is prominently displayed on the title page, satisfying the formatting criterion and orienting the reader immediately to the research focus.
The research question is stated verbatim on the title page and is sharply focused, clearly framing the investigation for the reader.
The Ansoff Matrix is clearly introduced with definitions and citations, showing accurate use of strategic terminology.
Only the market penetration quadrant is fully developed; other quadrants are described but lack equivalent detail, reducing methodological balance.
The strategic examples for market development and product development demonstrate thoughtful application of the Ansoff framework.
The student applies financial terminology accurately when explaining the position map axes, reflecting clear conceptual understanding.
Figure 1 lacks clear numerical scales on its axes, making it difficult for the reader to verify specific risk and return values visually.
The caption for Figure 1 is purely descriptive; it should include analytical commentary relating key data points back to the research question.
The discussion on page 7 explicitly ties the visual evidence to the research question by highlighting ARKKâs comparative underperformance.
The claim that ARKKâs underperformance may be temporary is unsubstantiated; it requires supporting data or literature to validate this alternative perspective.
Equations are correctly formatted in LaTeX and accompanied by clear definitions of each variable, demonstrating strong conceptual clarity.
The theoretical description of meanâvariance optimization is accurate but no sample calculations or data are provided to illustrate its practical application.
The list numbering is inconsistent (skipping item (2)), which distracts from the otherwise clear presentation of steps.
The student critically evaluates key assumptions of MPT, demonstrating awareness of its limitations in real-world markets.
The Efficient Frontier graph lacks clear numeric labels on axes and data points, reducing its utility for precise analysis.
The discussion could be strengthened by contrasting MPT with alternative portfolio theories to deepen critical evaluation.
The step-by-step outline for selecting stocks and computing variances/covariances provides a clear roadmap for practical implementation.
The discussion of diversification ends mid-sentence, indicating truncation and interrupting the assessment of this key quadrant.
The conclusion clearly restates findings and answers the research question with the phrase âto a great extent,â demonstrating strong synthesis.
While limitations are acknowledged, the conclusion does not propose suggestions for future research or practical next steps.
The Works Cited list demonstrates extensive research, drawing on a wide range of academic and industry sources.
Citation formats are inconsistent across entries (mixed MLA, APA, web formats), which undermines the professional presentation of references.
Several online sources lack access dates, which is required for proper referencing of web materials.
The table of contents uses a clear hierarchical structure of sections and subsections, aiding the readerâs understanding of the reportâs organization.
The Introduction effectively situates the topic by combining personal motivation with ARKKâs business context, providing a compelling rationale for the study.
The table of contents contains duplicate entries (e.g., repeated âIntroductionâ and âWorks Citedâ), which detracts from navigability and professional presentation.
No sensitivity analysis or scenario testing is discussed, limiting the depth of risk-return evaluation.
The narrative relies heavily on personal anecdote, which reduces the academic tone and limits the integration of scholarly sources at this stage.
The sentence on capital outflows ends abruptly and lacks completion, disrupting the flow and suggesting an OCR or editing oversight.
The introduction on page 4 clearly links the use of a position map and the Ansoff matrix to the research question, reinforcing methodological coherence.
The use of first-person pronouns (âI personally am an investorâ) undermines the formal academic register expected in an extended essay.
The Scope & Methodology section clearly defines the theoretical framework and business tools, demonstrating focused planning aligned to the research question.
The methodology does not justify the choice of a three-year risk horizon or explain why specific metrics were selected, limiting evidence of informed selection.
